action.reasoning: Observation: the ResNet switch failed immediately because torchvision tried to download pretrained weights, and network is disabled. Analysis: we need an offline-only backbone. The cached CLIP models are guaranteed available offline, and the previous failures likely came from incorrect loading/training logic rather than unavailable weights. A safe recovery is to use CLIP as a frozen feature extractor with a trainable linear head only; this avoids fragile full fine-tuning, should learn quickly, and keeps predict/train consistent. Decision: rewrite model/train/predict to an offline CLIP linear-probe pipeline, add a tiny overfit mode to verify learning, then run a short 1-epoch sanity overfit on 512 samples and local val eval. If train accuracy rises well above random, we can scale next iteration.
action.command: python train.py --epochs 1 --batch_size 256 --num_workers 8 --lr 5e-3 --weight_decay 1e-4 --freeze_backbone --train_subset 512 --val_subset 2048 --save_path checkpoints/sanity_clip_probe.pt && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/sanity_clip_probe.pt --eval_split val --batch_size 256 --num_workers 8
